feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: I Never Got To Apologize To My Sister Before She Died. Then I Found A Life-Changing Slip Of Paper In Her Desk.
entry.summary: "There were so many things that were left unsaid, but it’s the questions I can’t ask her anymore that haunt me the most.”

entry.title: Fish Oil Supplements May Not Help Stave Off Dementia After All
entry.summary: A large-scale clinical trial has shown that even long-term consumption of DHA—an omega-3 fatty acid found in abundance in oily fish—may not lead to improvements in cognitive function.

entry.title: Scientists discover the deep sleep circuit that builds muscle, burns fat, and boosts the brain
entry.summary: Researchers have identified the brain circuitry that links deep sleep with the release of growth hormone, revealing how the two regulate each other. The newly discovered feedback loop helps explain why poor sleep can interfere with growth, muscle repair, fat …

entry.title: Psychologist Roy Baumeister ran a large study on what separates a happy life from a meaningful one, and the divide is sharper than you'd expect: happiness comes from getting what you want, but meaning comes from what you give, whether you spend your d
entry.summary: In 2013, the psychologist Roy Baumeister and three colleagues published a paper with a plain title: “Some Key Differences between a Happy Life and a Meaningful Life.” They surveyed 397 adults and found something that cuts against the obvious assumption. Happi…

entry.title: She Was Warned About a Severe Food Allergy at a Large Party. Why She Is Completely Refusing to Change Her Recipe.
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: You don’t have to run to get fitter—this 20-minute treadmill walking workout is a great cardio session that’s gentle on your joints
entry.summary: Add a low-impact challenge to your day in just 20 minutes

entry.title: 5 simple habits for heart that can actually protect your arteries and prevent plaque build up
entry.summary: Dr. Andrew Freeman, a cardiologist at National Jewish Health in Denver, told TODAY.com the best habit isn't complicated. Roll out of bed, put on gym clothes, and just get it done before the day starts. "You don't have to worry about it," he said. He pushes ba…

entry.title: Millionaire Bryan Johnson opens up about autoimmune disease damaging his stomach, says ‘Hard to detect, o
entry.summary: Tech News News: Anti-aging influencer and millionaire Bryan Johnson has recently disclosed that he is battling an autoimmune disease called autoimmune gastritis (AIG).

entry.title: Two People With Severe Autoimmune Disease Are in Remission After 'Reset' Treatment
entry.summary: The severe and aggressive autoimmune disease known as neuromyelitis optica (NMO) just met a new match.

entry.title: Food-borne parasite infections triple in Michigan. What you should do
entry.summary: Officials say businesses and individuals need to take steps to reduce risk of infection from produce.

entry.title: 8 Easy Ways To Help Prevent Dementia if It Runs in Your Family
entry.summary: If you have a loved one who has suffered from dementia, you probably wish you could look into a crystal ball and know if you, too, will experience memory loss. But there’s reason to feel empowered, not fearful. There are everyday habits that can protect your …

entry.title: Erectile Dysfunction May Be an Early Sign of Disease, Experts Warn
entry.summary: Erectile dysfunction is a topic many are reluctant to discuss, but flagging the problem with a doctor could be important.

entry.title: Scientists Just Unlocked an Endless Army of Cancer-Fighting Cells
entry.summary: The human immune system has evolved an incredible ability to fight off cancerous cells.

entry.title: Here's How Long You Should Spend In The Bathroom When Pooping
entry.summary: Doctors share the ideal time everyone should be spending on the toilet — and what you can do to improve your bowel health.

entry.title: Liver cancer doesn't always announce itself: The early symptoms many people miss
entry.summary: The liver is one of the hardest-working organs in the human body. It filters toxins, helps digest food, stores nutrients, regulates metabolism, and supports immunity. Yet, despite its importance, it often suffers in silence.Liver cancer is now among the leadi…

entry.title: 3 "Bad" Snacks That Actually Support Heart Health
entry.summary: Not all “bad” snacks are actually bad for your heart. Learn which dietitian-approved picks can support a heart-healthy eating pattern.

entry.title: A chair may be one of the most dangerous places for your heart: Cardiologist explains the risks of sitting too long
entry.summary: A typical workday now begins with a laptop and often ends with one. Between office meetings, traffic, television, and scrolling on phones, many people spend most of their waking hours sitting down. It feels harmless because sitting doesn't hurt. There is no p…

entry.title: Peptide That Makes You Tan Linked to Skin Cancer, Doctor Warns
entry.summary: A surgeon is warning about the cancer risks associated with a popular gray-market tanning peptide, Melanotan II.

entry.title: A Dietitian Shares 10 Foods People Think Are Healthy but Are Loaded With Sugar
entry.summary: Some “healthy” foods are surprisingly high in sugar. Our dietitian reveals foods that seem healthy but are loaded with sugar.

entry.title: Why scratching a mosquito bite makes it so much worse, according to science
entry.summary: Scratching activates mast cells that release histamine and substance P, creating a prolonged itch-scratch cycle that worsens skin inflammation.

entry.title: Move over Chutes & Ladders: Schisto & Ladders has educational value plus worms
entry.summary: Spread by parasitic worms that can infect via a splash of water, schistosomiasis causes debilitating symptoms. It hits kids hard. A board game teaches how to prevent it.

entry.title: Sudden craving of 3 foods 'could be early sign of dementia'
entry.summary: The Alzheimer's Society has highlighted that changes in eating habits, including sudden cravings for sweet, fatty foods or carbohydrates, may be early warning signs of frontotemporal dementia

entry.title: The Best Drink for Joint Health, Backed by Registered Dietitians and Science
entry.summary: See the best drink you can sip on for joint health, according to registered dietitians. This healthy beverage is packed with antioxidants, and also benefits your heart, more.

entry.title: Legionnaires cluster emerging on Upper East Side, NYC officials say
entry.summary: At least 10 people have been infected and officials are still looking for the cause.

entry.title: Age at which childhood abuse occurs is associated with distinct brain activity in adulthood
entry.summary: A new study in Molecular Psychiatry suggests that the timing of childhood abuse is linked to distinct differences in how the adult brain processes emotional information, depending on whether the trauma happened in early childhood or adolescence.

entry.title: The US is hooked on unregulated peptides. But are they effective, or even safe?
entry.summary: The world of peptides has exploded in wellness circles, but the benefits of injecting these gray-market molecules rest on little clinical evidence.

entry.title: Harvard gastroenterologist reveals 7 silent symptoms of pancreatic cancer everyone should know
entry.summary: "Awareness is the first step in prevention and early action." The quote may sound simple, but when it comes to pancreatic cancer, it carries enormous weight.Pancreatic cancer is one of the most challenging cancers to diagnose early. The pancreas, a small orga…

entry.title: 5-Year-Old’s Terminal Diagnosis Leads to Baby Brother's Life-Saving Treatment for the Same Disease
entry.summary: "You’re saving your baby, but you’re losing your baby, too," said mom Millie Grennan of her children, who both have MLD, often described as childhood dementi...

entry.title: What Happens to Your Blood Sugar When You Eat Cherries, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Registered dietitians share what happens to your blood sugar when you eat cherries, along with tips for managing blood sugar.

entry.title: As Pakistan’s monsoon arrives, people with HIV worry
entry.summary: Dialogue Earth says after the devastating 2025 floods, people with HIV are increasingly vulnerable.

entry.title: Why Americans are living longer again
entry.summary: US life expectancy is on track for a record high in 2025 as the death rate hit its lowest level ever recorded, CDC data shows. Here’s why.

entry.title: Shoulder pain leads to Texas dad's cancer diagnosis: "I was like, 'Wait, what?'"
entry.summary: Eric Dillon thought the pain in his shoulder was a minor injury. It took two years to get the real answer.

entry.title: Metformin and cancer: active surveillance needed
entry.summary: Metformin failed in prostate cancer and many others—but exceptions remind us to think with precision about interventions

entry.title: The Best High-Fiber Breakfast for the Mediterranean Diet, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Dietitians say overnight oats are the best high-fiber breakfast for the Mediterranean diet. Learn why they love it, plus other alternatives.

entry.title: Wastewater wars: Massachusetts company loses state contract as public health evolves
entry.summary: Biobot proved waste water surveillance was valuable enough that governments now want to do it themselves.

entry.title: Could We Have Been Wrong About Fish Oil and Brain Health? New Study Raises Major Questions
entry.summary: Fish oil supplements delivered omega-3s to the brain, but a new study found that was not enough to improve cognitive health in older adults at risk for Alzheimer’s.

entry.title: More tick bites are happening closer to home
entry.summary: Experts are sharing warnings about the risk of ticks in Pennsylvania during the summer months. Researchers say the climate of two seasons impacts the tick population the most.

entry.title: The Brand Behind That 'Botox In A Bottle' Serum Makes A Beloved Hair Growth Serum, Too
entry.summary: We asked a dermatologist to explain the science behind this effective ingredient.

entry.title: Food Preservatives May Increase the Risk of High Blood Pressure and Cardiovascular Disease
entry.summary: Aa large-scale study demonstrates that preservatives widely used in everyday processed foods may exacerbate common health risks.

entry.title: This super-cooled squirrel could revolutionise emergency care
entry.summary: No other mammal can survive colder body temperatures than the Arctic ground squirrel. Its chilly hibernation is inspiring new treatments for heart attacks, stroke, and brain injury.

entry.title: What to know about cyclosporiasis as Michigan health officials investigate 300+ cases in outbreak
entry.summary: Over 300 cases of cyclosporiasis have been reported within a week in Southeast Michigan, and Michigan health officials are investigating.

entry.title: The Mysterious Civilization That Pre-Dated The Incas by 500 Years
entry.summary: The mysterious Inca Empire is a source of endless fascination to many, representing the largest and most elaborate society of pre-Columbian America.

entry.title: Doctors Say There’s An Undeniable Link Between Eye Health And Dementia. Here’s What You Need To Know.
entry.summary: Just one of many reasons why routine appointments for this body part are so important.

entry.title: Vagus Nerve Stimulation Has a Profound Impact on Severe Depression, Major Trial Finds
entry.summary: Depression affects more than 300 million of us, and sometimes, treatment works.

entry.title: 5 Things to Avoid After 5 P.M. If You Have High Blood Pressure
entry.summary: Learn about the 5 things experts recommend avoiding after 5 p.m. if you have high blood pressure. Some things to be avoided include alcohol, sugary snacks and too much screen time.

entry.title: 30-Minute Dinners to Improve Your Gut Health (Weekly Plan & Shopping List!)
entry.summary: Discover quick, delicious dinners curated by a dietitian that are designed to support gut health, featuring prebiotic and probiotic-rich ingredients.

entry.title: US Life Expectancy On Track To Reach Record High
entry.summary: The US age-adjusted death rate fell to a record low in 2025, likely pushing life expectancy to a record high as overdose deaths declined and mortality improved across all age groups. CNN reports: There were about 689 deaths for every 100,000 people in the US…

entry.title: Scientists discover why some brains resist Alzheimer's
entry.summary: Some brains appear to fight back against Alzheimer's by helping immature brain cells survive damage instead of succumbing to it. Understanding this natural resilience could point researchers toward entirely new ways to protect memory and slow dementia.

entry.title: More than 60 North Carolina cases tied to parasite behind 'explosive diarrhea'
entry.summary: North Carolina is dealing with a rise in Cyclospora cases, a microscopic parasite known for unpleasant gastrointestinal symptoms, including "explosive diarrhea."

entry.title: Prehistoric Plague Could Have Caused a Population Collapse in Stone Age Europe
entry.summary: A new study reveals that plague was lethal thousands of years ago, during the Neolithic.
